---
title: "Friends"
format: html
---

## Reflecting on Decisions: Time with Friends

By paying more attention to the decision making process, you will have a greater chance to interrupt the chain of decisions that could lead to a relapse. This is important because it is much easier to stop the process early, before you wind up in a high-risk situation, than later when you are in a situation that is harder to handle and may expose you to a number of triggers. Some times these decisions do not seem directly relevant to drinking, but nonetheless move one closer to drinking. <!--reference irrelevant_decisions.qmd-->


::: callout
### Reflect on the following questions:

- Are there certain friends or social situations that increase your urge to drink or use substances?

- What thoughts, feelings, or pressures tend to come up when spending time with these friends?

- Have you noticed any seemingly irrelevant decisions that make it more likely you will end up in risky social situations?

- What warning signs tell you that a social situation may be becoming difficult for your recovery?

- What boundaries, coping strategies, or alternative plans could help you maintain your recovery goals while staying socially connected?
:::

![](https://github.com/jjcurtin/aud_support/blob/main/modules/images/friendship3.png?raw=true){fig-align="center"}
